food_subgroup: Mixed fried snack platter — meat balls, fried chicken wings, and chicken cutlet

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a mixed Thai snack platter. FOODON_03315116 (chicken wing food product) is used as the closest parent anchor for the dominant fried chicken component. Beef/chicken ball and cutlet components are not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Poultry meat, cooked) is the closest defensible parent for the dominant poultry components (chicken wings, drumettes, cutlet). The mixed-platter nature and beef/chicken ball components are not individually represented in FoodEx2 at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps to a mixed Thai fried snack platter of this composition.

preparation_method: grilled (wings/drumettes may be grilled or fried)

serving.description: 1 box (approximately 4 pcs beef or chicken ball + 4 pcs chicken wing & drumette + 1 crispy chicken cutlet)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 350 g, comprising four meat balls (beef or chicken), four pieces of chicken wing and drumette, and one crispy chicken cutlet, as served in the Snack Delight Box C at Saap Saap Thai.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, cooked, fried (SR Legacy 05062); Chicken cutlet, breaded and fried (SR Legacy 05295); Beef meatball, cooked (SR Legacy 13356); composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for Snack Delight Box C or an equivalent Thai mixed snack platter in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries: fried chicken wing (USDA 05062, ~35% weight), fried chicken drumette (USDA 05062 proxy, ~25% weight), breaded fried chicken cutlet (USDA 05295, ~25% weight), and cooked beef/chicken meatball (USDA 13356 proxy, ~15% weight). Weights are proportional estimates based on described portion composition. Values represent a plausible central estimate for this fried mixed-meat snack platter.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate based on fried chicken and breaded meat products (GI ~40–50 range).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Snack Delight Box C. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables for analogous fried and breaded meat products (fried chicken, breaded cutlets, meat balls). These foods have low-to-medium GI (approximately 40–50) due to protein and fat content slowing glucose absorption. The carbohydrate contribution is primarily from the batter/breadcrumb coating. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (45 × 29.4 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving at 8.4 g/100g) / 100 ≈ 10.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100 g; ~1680 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish, as a single serving provides approximately 1680 mg sodium — over 70% of the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg — primarily from seasoning, marinades, and processed meat balls.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from batter/breadcrumb coating with low-to-medium GI; high fat content may impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in moderation: the low GI (45) limits acute glycaemic spikes, but the high fat content (~57 g fat per serving) and caloric density (~868 kcal per serving) may contribute to weight gain and impaired insulin sensitivity over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and processed meat components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid frequent consumption of this dish, as deep-frying contributes significant saturated fat (~15 g per serving), trans fat (~0.7 g per serving), and cholesterol (~273 mg per serving), alongside high sodium, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Snack Delight Box C is a Thai-inspired mixed fried snack platter served at Saap Saap Thai restaurant in Singapore. The box contains four pieces of beef or chicken ball, four pieces of chicken wing and drumette, and one crispy chicken cutlet — a combination designed for sharing or as a substantial individual snack. The chicken wings and drumettes are typically marinated in Thai spices and deep-fried to a golden crisp, while the chicken cutlet is breaded and fried for a crunchy exterior with juicy interior. The meat balls (beef or chicken) are seasoned with Thai aromatics and cooked through. The overall flavour profile is savoury, spicy, and umami-rich, reflecting Thai culinary influences including lemongrass, fish sauce, and chilli. Nutritionally, this is a high-protein, high-fat, calorie-dense dish. Per 100 g it provides approximately 248 kcal, 18.5 g protein, 16.2 g fat, and 8.4 g carbohydrate. A full serving (~350 g) delivers roughly 868 kcal. The sodium content is high (~1680 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at 45 (low), as the carbohydrate content is primarily from batter and breadcrumbs, and the high protein and fat content slows glucose absorption. Despite the low GI, the caloric density and saturated fat content make this dish unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It is not vegetarian, not vegan, and contains gluten from the batter. Halal status is unconfirmed from available data.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Snack Delight Box C exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the individual components (fried chicken wing, breaded fried chicken cutlet, cooked meatball). The composition proportions are estimated from the described serving contents. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable fried and breaded meat products. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken wing (FDCID 05062), breaded fried chicken cutlet (FDCID 05295), and cooked beef meatball (FDCID 13356), weighted by approximate portion proportions. No direct approved-source entry exists for this dish. 2. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate based on the described contents (4 meat balls ~80 g, 4 chicken wing/drumette pieces ~160 g, 1 chicken cutlet ~110 g). 3. Halal status is listed as false/unconfirmed; Saap Saap Thai's halal certification status was not verifiable from approved sources at time of processing. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the composite USDA SR Legacy sources used. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite sources used. 7. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 8. Trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g) is estimated from deep-frying in commercial oils; value is approximate. 9. The cuisine_tags 'Pad Thai' and 'Thai Noodle' from the input record are not applicable to this dish and have been excluded from the output as they are field survey platform tags unrelated to the food composition.
